records.ety: [L. sycomorus, Gr. <?/ the fig mulberry; <?/ a fig + <?/ the black mulberry; or perhaps of Semitic origin: cf. F. sycomore. Cf. Mulberry.]

defs: A large tree (Ficus Sycomorus) allied to the common fig. It is found in Egypt and Syria, and is the sycamore, or sycamine, of Scripture.
The American plane tree, or buttonwood.
A large European species of maple (Acer Pseudo-Platanus).
